_comment: Catalog of common Windows system-tray icons categorized by our recommendation. The tray-taskbar module reads current tray visibility from HKCU:\Control Panel\NotifyIconSettings (Win11 24H2+) or HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\NotifyIconSettings (older). Match on executablePath basename OR displayName substring (case-insensitive). Categories: keep_visible (essential system indicators — do not hide), hide_often (bloat / low-value nag icons), decide_per_user (personal preference — ask user).
_matchRule: Some icons are lazily registered (only appear after their app has been open once). Enumerate the persistent registry list, then overlay whatever's currently in the running tray. If an icon is registered but the exe doesn't exist any more, propose cleanup of the stale entry.

keep_visible.reason: Only appears when a restart is pending — keep visible.

hide_often.reason: Cortana is deprecated for consumer use.
Legacy Skype Meet Now button — rarely used.
Overlay tray hint — hide unless the user actively games.
Setup-nag icon (NOT the sync client itself). See startup_disable_safe.json — the actual sync client OneDrive.exe is on the tripwire list and stays.

decide_per_user.reason: GPU tuning — keep if you tweak per-app settings; hide if you set-and-forget.
Optimize + capture — hide if you never use ShadowPlay.
Radeon Software — same trade as NVIDIA CP.
Audio jack detection + EQ — most users never open it.
Sync status — hide if you don't monitor uploads.
Sync status — hide if you don't monitor uploads.
Unread badge — many users want it, some don't.
Meeting reminders — hide if you launch Zoom via calendar links only.
Password manager — usually keep for quick unlock, but tray isn't strictly required.
OEM utility — most users can hide.
OEM utility — most users can hide.
OEM utility — most users can hide.
